What is BSIS?: BSIS stands for Bachelor of Science in Information Systems. It is a four-year degree program that blends business and computing to prepare students for tech-related roles in organizations.
How many years is the BSIS program?: The BSIS program typically takes four (4) academic years to complete.
What are the core subjects of BSIS?: Core BSIS subjects include Programming, Systems Analysis, Database Management, Web Development, E-Commerce, and Project Management.
What kind of jobs can I get after gradua: BSIS graduates can work as Systems Analysts, IT Project Managers, Business Analysts, Web Developers, Database Administrators, or IT Consultants.
Is BSIS more on programming?: BSIS includes programming but also focuses on business processes, systems analysis, and IT solutions for organizations.
Do we have a school uniform?: Yes, ZDSPGC requires students to wear proper school uniforms as part of the dress code policy.
Is BSIS a CHED-recognized program?: Yes, the BSIS program offered at ZDSPGC - Aurora is recognized by CHED (Commission on Higher Education).
Is there a thesis in BSIS?: Yes, BSIS students are required to complete a Capstone Project or Thesis during their final year.
Are there math subjects in BSIS?: Yes, BSIS includes subjects like Business Math, Statistics, and Quantitative Techniques.
Is there OJT (On-the-Job Training) in BS: Yes, students are required to undergo OJT in their senior year as part of the curriculum.
When is the enrollment period?: Enrollment usually starts a few weeks before the semester begins. Please check with the registrar for exact dates.
What documents are needed to enroll?: Common requirements include your Form 138 (Report Card), PSA Birth Certificate, Certificate of Good Moral Character, and 2x2 photos.
Can I enroll online?: Currently, most transactions are done onsite. Online enrollment is not yet available.
Where is the BSIS building located?: The BSIS building is within the main campus. Ask any school staff for directions once you arrive.
Do we have internet access in the comput: Yes, the computer lab has internet access for research and system development use.
How many units per semester in BSIS?: A typical semester ranges from 18 to 24 units depending on your curriculum flowchart.
What is the grading system?: The school uses the standard grading system: 1.0 (Excellent) to 5.0 (Fail).
What is NSTP?: NSTP stands for National Service Training Program, and it's a required subject for all first-year college students.
Who teaches Programming 1?: Programming 1 is taught by Prof. John Bon Juvi Jumarito.
Is Computer Programming hard?: Programming can be challenging at first, but with practice and proper guidance, students usually adapt well.
Can I shift to another course later?: Yes, but you must consult the registrar and meet the requirements of your new desired course.
Do we have Saturday classes?: It depends on your subject schedule. Some classes may be scheduled on Saturdays.
How much is the tuition fee for BSIS?: As a government-funded college, tuition is usually free under TES and RA 10931 (Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act).
What is the TES program?: TES stands for Tertiary Education Subsidy, a financial assistance program for qualified students.
Can I use a laptop in class?: Yes, students are encouraged to use laptops especially during programming and system-related activities.
What programming language do we learn fi: The first programming language introduced is Python or Java, depending on the instructor.
Do we have group projects?: Yes, especially in subjects like Systems Analysis, Capstone Project, and Web Development.
Is attendance important?: Yes, regular attendance is required and monitored by instructors.
Do we have PE subjects?: Yes, Physical Education (PE) is a general requirement during your first two years.
What are minor subjects in BSIS?: Minor subjects include Communication, Filipino, Math, PE, NSTP, and others not specific to the major.
Can I join clubs or organizations?: Yes, students are encouraged to join school clubs such as ICT Society or cultural clubs.
Where can I ask if I have a problem with: You can talk to your subject instructor or visit the program head or guidance counselor.
Do we have midterms and finals?: Yes, each semester includes midterm and final examinations.
Are we allowed to drop a subject?: Yes, dropping a subject is allowed under certain conditions and within a specified deadline.
Can I take summer classes?: Yes, summer classes are offered depending on the subject demand and schedule availability.
Do we have a library?: Yes, the ZDSPGC library is available to all students for research and study.
Do we use LMS or Google Classroom?: Some instructors use Google Classroom or LMS platforms depending on their preference.
What if I fail a subject?: You will need to retake the subject in the following semester.
Do we have activities and events?: Yes, the school holds intramurals, IT Week, seminars, and student organization events.
Can I request a TOR after one semester?: A Transcript of Records is usually issued after completing at least one academic year.
Is there a scholarship available?: Yes, TES and other government-funded scholarships are available to qualified students.
Do we have free Wi-Fi on campus?: Yes, there are areas in the campus with free Wi-Fi access for students.
Can I choose my own schedule?: Schedules are usually pre-arranged but students can sometimes choose based on available slots.
How many hours per day is class?: Classes usually run 3 to 6 hours per day depending on your schedule.
Is the BSIS building air-conditioned?: Some rooms are air-conditioned, especially computer labs.
Do we have field trips or industry visit: Yes, industry exposure through field trips or seminars is part of the BSIS curriculum.
What is the passing grade?: The passing grade for most subjects is 75%.
Where can I get my ID?: Your student ID is issued by the registrar’s office after you complete enrollment.
How to contact the registrar's office?: You can contact the registrar by visiting their office during school hours or through the official ZDSPGC Facebook page.
What should I do if I lose my ID?: If you lose your student ID, report it to the registrar’s office immediately and request for a replacement.
Is there a dress code in the campus?: Yes, students are required to wear their uniforms or follow the proper dress code policy of the school.
Where is the canteen located?: The school canteen is located near the center of the campus, close to the main building.
Are there rest areas or study lounges?: Yes, there are designated rest areas and shaded spots where students can study or relax.
Do we have a guidance counselor?: Yes, the school has a guidance counselor available for student concerns and academic advice.
What if I have no laptop for programming: You can use the computer labs provided by the BSIS department for programming tasks.
How can I join the ICT club?: Ask any BSIS instructor about the ICT club or look out for recruitment posters on campus.
Are there school events for BSIS student: Yes, BSIS students have dedicated events like IT Week, seminars, and project exhibits.
Is there a code of conduct?: Yes, ZDSPGC follows a student handbook which includes the code of conduct and disciplinary actions.
Do freshmen need to attend orientation?: Yes, orientation is important as it introduces you to the school policies, instructors, and the BSIS program.
Is attendance to orientation mandatory?: Yes, it is highly encouraged and often mandatory to attend the orientation for new students.
What if I miss an exam?: If you miss an exam, inform your instructor as soon as possible and provide a valid reason to request a special exam.
Do we need to buy textbooks?: Some instructors provide digital materials, but others may require specific textbooks or references.
Can I shift from BSIS to BSIT or BSED?: Yes, shifting is allowed based on your academic standing and subject evaluation.
What is a Capstone Project?: A Capstone Project is a major project completed in your final year where you apply your knowledge to solve real-world problems.
Is BSIS offered in other ZDSPGC campuses: BSIS is mainly offered in the Aurora campus; availability in other campuses may vary.
How many students are in one section?: A typical BSIS section consists of 30 to 40 students.
What is a curriculum flowchart?: It is a guide that shows the sequence and structure of subjects to be taken every semester.
Do we have computer literacy subjects?: Yes, Introduction to Computing and Computer Programming 1 cover computer literacy and foundational IT skills.
What are the electives in BSIS?: Electives may include Mobile App Development, Data Science, or Advanced Web Technologies depending on availability.
Is there a computer club?: Yes, the ICT Club is the main student organization for computer enthusiasts in BSIS.
How often do we have programming classes: Programming classes are held weekly and include both lecture and hands-on lab sessions.
Do we have laboratory exams?: Yes, laboratory-based exams are part of programming and computer-related subjects.
Can I take extra units?: Yes, with permission from the dean or registrar, you can enroll in additional units if eligible.
What is the maximum load per semester?: The maximum load usually ranges between 21 to 24 units.
Is there a student handbook?: Yes, the student handbook is given during orientation or can be requested from the registrar.
What is the grading policy for group wor: Group work is graded based on individual contribution and the group’s output quality.
Are freshmen allowed to enter the comput: Freshmen can access the computer lab during their scheduled class or with permission during vacant hours.
Do instructors use Google Meet or Zoom?: Instructors may use Google Meet or Zoom for online consultations or blended classes.
Can I request a tutorial from instructor: Yes, instructors usually offer consultation hours for tutorials or clarifications.
Is it hard to pass Programming 1?: It can be challenging, but consistent practice and attending lab sessions help a lot.
Do we have printing services in school?: Yes, there are printing services near or within the campus that students can use.
How are class schedules announced?: Schedules are posted on bulletin boards or announced through the school’s official Facebook group.
Can I use phones in class?: Phones are allowed for educational purposes with the instructor’s permission.
Do we need to memorize code?: Memorizing code isn't required, but understanding logic and syntax is important.
What is the passing grade in Programming: The passing grade in most subjects including Programming is 75%.
Is there a school app?: Currently, there is no dedicated school app, but updates are posted on social media and official platforms.
How do I check my grades?: Grades are released by instructors or through the registrar’s office.
Can I graduate earlier than 4 years?: Graduating earlier is uncommon unless the school offers accelerated classes.
Are there math competitions in BSIS?: Not specific to BSIS, but you may join inter-departmental competitions held annually.
Can I switch sections if I have conflict: Yes, with approval from the program head and registrar.
Are there night classes?: Some classes may be held in the late afternoon, but full night classes are rare for freshmen.
What happens if I don’t wear a uniform?: You may not be allowed to enter the classroom or may face disciplinary action.
Do I need a student email?: Yes, the school may provide a student email or require one for academic use.
Can I access past exam papers?: Some instructors may share past exams for review; otherwise, you may ask upperclassmen for study tips.
